memoryArtifacts.description: photograph of 14th century stained glass window, possibly portraying John Cherleton (1268 to 1353) in St Mary's Church, Shrewsbury, England. It was originally in the Greyfriars. The knight is bearing the arms of Powys and is probably Cherleton

events.name: accompanied King Edward to France for his wedding
acquired Pole castle (today's Welshpool) on his marriage
built the basis of the present Powis Castle
arrested and executed his rival, the Earl of Arundel, under the orders of the Queen

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John de Cherleton 1st Lord Cherleton於1268年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾萊可沃迪時，他的父親Robert Cherleton14歲，母親Elinor Holland14歲。他於1309年7月26日與Hawise de la Pole在英國英格蘭史洛普爾萊可沃迪結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1309年登記服兵役。他去世於1353年12月21日享年85歲，埋葬在英國史洛普爾史福斯波利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John de Cherleton 1st Lord Cherleton于1268年出生在英国英格兰史洛普爾萊可沃迪时，他的父亲Robert Cherleton14岁，母亲Elinor Holland14岁。他于1309年7月26日与Hawise de la Pole在英国英格兰史洛普爾萊可沃迪结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1309年登记服兵役。他去世于1353年12月21日享年85岁，埋葬在英国史洛普爾史福斯波利。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cherleton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Charlton
meanings: English (Northumberland and Durham): habitational name from North Charlton or Charlton near Bellingham (both Northumberland) or from any of the many other places so named (there are over ninety examples in England), from Old English Ceorlatūn ‘settlement (Old English tūn) of the peasants’. Old English ceorl denoted originally a free peasant of the lowest rank, later (but probably already before the Norman conquest) a tenant in pure villeinage, a serf or bondsman.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John de Cherleton 1st Lord Cherleton was born in 1268, in Wrockwardine, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Robert Cherleton, was 14 and his mother, Elinor Holland, was 14. He married Hawise de la Pole on 26 July 1309, in Wrockwardine, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He registered for military service in 1309. He died on 21 December 1353, at the age of 85, and was buried in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John de Cherleton 1st Lord Cherleton est né en 1268, à Wrockwardine, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert Cherleton, avait 14 ans et sa mère, Elinor Holland, avait 14 ans. Il a épousé Hawise de la Pole le 26 juillet 1309, à Wrockwardine, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1309. Il est décédé le 21 décembre 1353, à l’âge de 85 ans, et a été enterré à Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John de Cherleton 1st Lord Cherleton 1268 in Wrockwardine, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert Cherleton 14 und seine Mutter Elinor Holland 14. Er hat am 26. Juli 1309 in Wrockwardine, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom Hawise de la Pole geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er hat sich 1309 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb am 21. Dezember 1353 im Alter von 85 Jahren und wurde in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John de Cherleton 1st Lord Cherleton è nato il nel 1268 nel Wrockwardine, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Robert Cherleton, aveva 14 anni e il suo madre, Elinor Holland, aveva 14 anni. Lui sposato Hawise de la Pole il 26 luglio 1309, nel Wrockwardine, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1309. Morì il 21 dicembre 1353, all'età di 85 anni, e fu sepolto a Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John de Cherleton 1st Lord Cherletonが1268年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアウロックワーディンで生まれた時，父Robert Cherletonは14歳，母Elinor Hollandは14歳であった。1309年7月26日にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアウロックワーディンでHawise de la Poleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1309年に兵役登録をした。彼は1353年12月21日に85歳で死去し，イギリスシュロプシュアイアシュリュースブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John de Cherleton 1st Lord Cherleton(이)가 1268년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 록워딘에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Cherleton(은)는 14세였고, 그의 어머니 Elinor Holland(은)는 14세였다. 그는 1309년 7월 26일 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 록워딘에서 Hawise de la Pole(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1309년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1353년 12월 21일 85세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 슈럽실르 슈레w스버이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John de Cherleton 1st Lord Cherleton nasceu em 1268, em Wrockwardine, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Robert Cherleton, tinha 14 anos e sua mãe, Elinor Holland, tinha 14 anos. Ele casou-se com Hawise de la Pole em 26 de julho de 1309, em Wrockwardine, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1309. Ele faleceu em 21 de dezembro de 1353, com 85 anos, e foi sepultado em Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John de Cherleton 1st Lord Cherleton родился в 1268 г. в местности рокквардаин, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Robert Cherleton было 14 лет, а матери, которую звали Elinor Holland, 14. Он женился на женщине по имени Hawise de la Pole 26 июля 1309 г. в местности рокквардаин, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. В 1309 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 21 декабря 1353 г. в возрасте 85 лет и был похоронен в местности шрюсбури, шропшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John de Cherleton 1st Lord Cherleton nació en 1268, en Wrockwardine, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Robert Cherleton, tenía 14 años y su mamá, Elinor Holland, tenía 14 años. Se casó con Hawise de la Pole el 26 de julio de 1309, en Wrockwardine, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1309. Murió el 21 de diciembre de 1353, a la edad de 85 años, y fue sepultado en Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra.
